other_observers: Mark Peterson (on all three days), Larry Semo (16 Dec); Brandon Percival found the bird in late Nov, I believe; many other observers during its stay
duration: at least 10 minutes over the three days
specific_location: Pueblo Reservoir, in SE corner and on tires at South Marina
description: A very large gull with nearly black mantle and wings. The head was white with indistinct gray-brown smudging on the crown. The yellow eyes appeard small. The bill was large and yellow with a distinct red spot on the gonys, which sported a fair angle.  The back and (folded) wings were all the same very dark gray (nearly black), except for the black outer primaries. There were neat and distinct white scapular and tertial crescents. The underparts were entirely white; the legs were a very dull, pale, grayish-pink. The tail was white.  In flight, the upper side of the wings looked nearly as black as the outer primaries (which were black). There was a medium-width white trailing edge. The outer three primaries were almost or wholly black except for the dark gray base to p9, the sharp white tips to p8-9, the relatively large white mirror on p9, and the connected white mirror and tip of p10.Behaviors: The bird was loafing in the water, on a small island, and on tires for most of the time of observation. I saw it fly a few times.Call: None heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
